Build your AI team · CETIN + Yettel Bulgaria

Pick your use case

Find your team, see your mission, and build your agent in Microsoft Copilot Studio — one step at a time.

👥 4 teams · pre-assigned 🤖 1 agent per team 🛠️ Copilot Studio
How the next hour works
The next hour is yours
🕐 Build your own
Each of you builds your own agent, on your own account — start to finish.
🪜 Step by step
One field at a time — Basic → Intermediate → Advanced. The guide walks you through it.
👥 You're in teams
Seated together — help each other as you go.
🧭 Build Coach on the floor
Stuck on anything? A Build Coach is right there — just ask.
Step 1 · find yourself
Tap your name to begin
🧩
Find your name under your team and tap it. Your use case is assigned — you'll only see your own build.
Your team

Your mission · build this agent
What's ahead
  • 🧭 Understand — your persona & what the agent does
  • 🎯 The end goal — what a finished agent returns
  • 🛠️ Build it — Setup → Basic → Intermediate → Advanced
  • 📣 Publish — share it to Teams when you're done
Yettel Meeting Prep · your persona
You are the CEO

You lead 4,200 people across CEE markets, mid-transformation to a digital connectivity platform. You walk into meetings across six functions with no time to prep — you need what matters, what's open, and what to say in under two minutes.

Role
Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
Company
Top-3 CEE telecom — Hungary, Bulgaria & other CEE markets
Direct reports
CCO · CFO · CPO · CTO · CLO · CSO
Reports to
Group CEO (Marcus Weber) · Group Board
Biggest challenge right now
4iG fixed-mobile convergence bundle launching Q4 — decision on the fixed-network partnership needed by Q3.
Meeting Prep · what the agent does
One outcome: walk in prepared

Tell the agent which meeting you're about to walk into — or ask what's on your calendar today. It returns a structured brief in under 60 seconds: who you're meeting, what's open, what you need to know, and how to open.

🗓️
1 · Reads your calendar
Finds the meeting — who, when, what it's about.
→
📧
2 · Searches your email
Recent threads, open items, commitments related to it.
→
📄
3 · Returns a brief
Context, recent activity, what to know, how to open.
📧
Every response is automatically emailed to your inbox — you don't need to ask, it always arrives.
Meeting Prep · data & knowledge sources
Three layers power the brief
DocumentWhat it containsUsed by
CEO_01_Strategic_ContextCompany vision, 2026 strategic priorities, cross-functional open items, external competitive context.Both agents
CEO_02_Leadership_TeamExCo profiles — roles, what each person owns, open items with each leader.Meeting Prep
CEO_03_ExCo_TrackerOpen decisions, pending actions, ExCo meeting agenda and status tracker.Meeting Prep
Work IQ — EmailLive threads: HR departure risk, CFO board prep, CTO overrun, legal/regulatory, strategy, commercial.Meeting Prep
Work IQ — CalendarUpcoming meetings: ExCo, 1:1 CHRO, Board Prep, Regulatory, Group CEO call, Vendor escalation.Meeting Prep
🧭
Uploaded docs + Work IQ (live email & calendar) = a brief grounded in what's actually happening.

Meeting Prep · build
Setup Before you start
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Open Copilot Studio
Go to copilotstudio.microsoft.com and sign in.
2
Check your environment
Confirm you're on the Acceler (default) environment — top-right corner.
3
Start right from the home screen
On “What would you like to build?”, paste your prompt directly and hit → — no need to create a new agent first. Only if you hit an issue: Agents → Create new agent.
🧭
Ahead: Stage 1 build from a prompt · Stage 2 connect your data & Work IQ · Stage 3 automate with a daily trigger.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 1 · Basic Start from a prompt
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Paste the starting prompt
On “What would you like to build?”, paste this into the box and hit →.
Build me a meeting preparation agent for a senior executive at a large telecom company. The agent should read my calendar to find upcoming meetings, search my emails for relevant context, and produce a structured brief telling me what I need to know before I walk in.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 1 · Basic Give it a few seconds
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Copilot builds your agent
It generates a name, description & instructions. “This feature isn't available until your agent has finished setting up” is normal — it takes a couple of seconds.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 1 · Basic Review what Copilot created
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Check name & description
Look at the Name, Description and Agent status. If the name isn't right, rename it to Executive Meeting Prep Assistant.
2
Sense-check the model
Copilot picks a model (e.g. Claude Sonnet 4.6) — leave it as is.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 1 · Basic Update the instructions
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Glance at what Copilot wrote
It usually drafts a reasonable meeting-prep agent. You'll replace it with the precise one now.
2
Open Instructions → replace
Replace the text with the full prompt below — note the auto-email line at the end. Click Save.
You are a personal briefing assistant for a senior leader at a leading telecom operator in Central and Eastern Europe (CEE). Your job is to prepare the leader for any upcoming meeting by pulling together everything they need to know — from their calendar, their emails, and the organisational knowledge sources. When the leader asks to be briefed before a meeting: 1. Check their calendar via Work IQ — find the meeting they are referring to 2. Search their emails via Work IQ — find threads related to the meeting topic, attendees, or subject matter 3. Search the knowledge sources — find relevant organisational context, open items, or strategic background 4. Synthesise everything into a structured pre-meeting brief Always structure your response as: Meeting — who, when, what it is about Context — background on this person, topic, or decision Recent Activity — what has happened recently in emails related to this meeting What You Need to Know — the 2-3 most important things to walk in with Recommended Opening — one suggested way to open the conversation Be direct and concise. Every point must be specific and grounded in actual data you find. At the end of every response, automatically send the full brief as an email to the user via Work IQ. Subject line: Meeting Brief — [meeting name]. Do not ask — always send.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 1 · Basic Test the basic agent
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Save, then open Test
Click Save, open the Test panel on the right.
2
Ask a general question
What should I know before a meeting with the national regulator?
3
Knowledge-only for now
No email or calendar yet — it answers from its own knowledge. Output should be structured.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 2 · Intermediate Add knowledge — choose SharePoint
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Knowledge → Add knowledge
Go to the Knowledge tab and click Add knowledge.
2
Pick SharePoint
In the dialog choose SharePoint (Powered by Work IQ) — not upload, not OneDrive.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 2 · Intermediate Add the three SharePoint links
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Paste each SharePoint URL
Use Enter URL of a SharePoint file or folder → Add → Add to agent. Each doc and its link:
CEO_01_Strategic_Context
https://acceler688-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/personal/ai_explorer_01_acceler688_onmicrosoft_com/IQCPBiXNC0LfR6lDo1hYTmmXAXUDiUJXfsAme1azG18hGDE?e=tbSoYB
CEO_02_Leadership_Team
https://acceler688-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/personal/ai_explorer_01_acceler688_onmicrosoft_com/IQAQB3KTsHu6Sbb7oPtTyC1oAUv_ZEpP1zO_oLqXcfLNJcA?e=ghKAuW
CEO_03_ExCo_Tracker
https://acceler688-my.sharepoint.com/:w:/g/personal/ai_explorer_01_acceler688_onmicrosoft_com/IQCAsHBh4StTTLCx4ALRINvDAbOMF522LgGPav-mIPx3Hvg?e=XqeZwK
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 2 · Intermediate Add to agent & let them index
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Add to agent
Each doc appears with a name & description — click Add to agent.
2
Wait for green / Ready
Each shows In progress, then a green Ready. Wait for all three before testing.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 2 · Intermediate Connect Work IQ — Mail
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Tools → Add a tool
Go to the Tools tab, click Add a tool.
2
Search for it
Type email mcp (or Work IQ Mail) in the search box.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 2 · Intermediate Add Work IQ Mail (Preview)
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Select & sign in
Pick Work IQ Mail (Preview), sign in with your Microsoft 365 account, then Add.
2
Reads your inbox only
It only reads your own emails. It now appears under Tools.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 2 · Intermediate Connect Work IQ — Calendar
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Add a second tool
Stay on Tools → Add a tool again. Search calendar mcp (or Work IQ Calendar).
2
Why calendar
Calendar lets the agent find your upcoming meetings automatically — that's what makes “Brief me before my next meeting” work.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 2 · Intermediate Add Work IQ Calendar (Preview)
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Select & add
Pick Work IQ Calendar (Preview), sign in if prompted, then Add.
2
Both tools now connected
Under Tools you should now see Work IQ Calendar + Work IQ Mail.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 2 · Intermediate Suggested prompts
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Set the toggle
Web search OFF — your sources only.
2
Add starter prompts
Suggested prompts → Add suggested prompts, then Save. Add these three — each is its own quick way in:
Prep me for my 1:1 with Miriam
What's on my calendar today?
Brief me on board prep with the CFO
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 2 · Intermediate Test with your real calendar
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Open Test & ask
Brief me before my Regulatory Meeting today
2
Allow the Work IQ connections
First run, it asks you to Connect to continue — Allow each: Work IQ User, Work IQ Copilot, Work IQ Mail.
3
Or try another meeting
Brief me before my Leadership Sync on Bulgaria Enterprise Strategy
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 2 · Intermediate What good looks like
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Grounded in real data
The brief references specific email threads, surfaces open items from your inbox, cross-references the knowledge docs, and suggests a concrete opening line.
2
It lands in your inbox
The brief also arrives as an email automatically.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 3 · Advanced Add a recurring trigger
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Triggers → Add trigger
On Overview, find Triggers → Add trigger. Select Recurrence, name it Daily Meeting Brief, click Next.
2
Set the schedule
Triggering interval = 1, Triggering frequency = Day.
3
Paste the trigger instruction
In Additional instructions… paste the prompt below, then Create trigger.
Check my calendar for today. Find all meetings scheduled for today. For each meeting, read relevant email threads and organisational context. Produce a structured daily brief covering all my meetings today and email it automatically.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 3 · Advanced Trigger created
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Time to test your trigger
You'll see “Your trigger was successfully added.” Click Close.
2
Check it's green
On Overview, the trigger shows a green status under Triggers.
Meeting Prep · build
Stage 3 · Advanced Verify & test
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Simulate the morning run
Open Test and ask — this is what the trigger does every morning:
Brief me before all my meetings today
2
Check your inbox
The daily brief lands on its own — before you open your laptop.
Yettel Market Pulse · your persona
Same CEO — a different problem

You're the same CEO — but markets move overnight. By the time something reaches you as a formal briefing, it's filtered and the raw signal is gone. Market Pulse gives you that raw signal, through your lens, before anyone else.

Role
Chief Executive Officer (CEO)
Markets
Hungary & Bulgaria (top-3 CEE operator)
Competitors in play
Magyar Telekom · 4iG · A1 · Vivacom · DIGI
Reports to
Group CEO (Marcus Weber) · Group Board
The job
Know what moved in your market — before anyone tells you — and what it means for your 2026 priorities.
Market Pulse · what the agent does
Know what moved before anyone tells you

You give it a market and a time window — "Hungary, last 7 days" — and it scans three signal layers, read through your strategic lens, and returns a structured brief.

🏁 External
Competitor pricing, network announcements, exec moves, press — live from the web.
⚖️ Regulatory
Spectrum decisions, competition rulings, consultations — flagged & interpreted.
📧 Internal
What your own team is already flagging in email — before you'd have seen it.
🎯
It asks before it runs. No market + time window? It stops and asks — "CEE, 30 days" is a different brief from "Hungary, 7 days." The discipline is the value.
Market Pulse · data & knowledge sources
Five sources power the brief
DocumentWhat it containsUsed by
CEO_01_Strategic_ContextCompany vision, 2026 priorities, cross-functional open items — frames signals in strategic context.Both agents
MP_01_Competitive_LandscapeMagyar Telekom, 4iG, A1, Vivacom, DIGI — recent moves, pricing, enterprise strategy, threat assessment.Market Pulse
MP_02_Regulatory_WatchSpectrum renewal consultation, Competition Authority review, GDPR audit, net-neutrality enforcement.Market Pulse
Work IQ — EmailInternal threads where your team has already flagged external signals.Stage 2+
Web Search (live)Real-time competitor press, regulatory announcements, market news — the live signal layer.Always
🌐
Web search is ON for Market Pulse — the live web is the signal layer, framed by your files.
Market Pulse · your VM
Everything's open in tabs

Across the top: Copilot Studio (where you build) · Mail & Calendar · the data tabs · Teams · and this guide. Click a tab to switch.
Market Pulse · your VM
Find Copilot Studio
🛠️
The Microsoft Copilot Studio tab is where you build — keep this guide open alongside it.
Market Pulse · what good looks like
One brief. Four sections.
📄 The brief structure
  • Headline — the single most important thing that moved.
  • What Moved — 3-5 developments: named actor, what, when, cited source.
  • What It Means For Us — tied to your 2026 priorities, by number.
  • What To Watch Next — 1-2 things in the next 7-14 days. Early warning.
  • Internal Signals (Stage 2+) — what your team already flagged.
The wow
"What It Means For Us" is the difference — the external signal interpreted through your lens and your 2026 priorities, not a generic news summary.
🏁
Competitors tracked: Magyar Telekom · 4iG · A1 · Vivacom · DIGI.
Market Pulse · build

SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Open Copilot Studio
Go to copilotstudio.microsoft.com and sign in.
2
Check your environment
Confirm you're on the Acceler (default) environment — top-right corner.
3
Start right from the home screen
On “What would you like to build?”, paste your prompt directly and hit → — no need to create a new agent first. Only if you hit an issue: Agents → Create new agent.

Ahead: Stage 1 build from a prompt · Stage 2 connect your data & Work IQ · Stage 3 automate with a daily trigger.
Market Pulse · build
Stage 1 · Basic Start from a prompt
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Paste the starting prompt
On “What would you like to build?”, paste this into the box and hit →.
Build me a market intelligence agent for a senior executive at a telecom company operating in Central and Eastern Europe. The agent should search the web for competitor moves, regulatory signals, and market news, and interpret what it finds through the company's strategic priorities.
Market Pulse · build
Stage 1 · Basic Give it a few seconds
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Copilot builds your agent
It generates a name, description & instructions. “Setting up…” is normal — it takes a couple of seconds to provision.
Market Pulse · build
Stage 1 · Basic Review what Copilot created
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Check name & description
Review the Name, Description and Instructions. If the name isn't right, rename to CEE Market Pulse.
2
Sense-check it
It should describe a market-intelligence agent that searches the web. You'll set the precise instructions next.
Market Pulse · build
Stage 1 · Basic Update the instructions
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Open Instructions → replace
Replace the text with the full prompt below — note the two-mode guardrail and the auto-email line. Click Save.
You are CEE Market Pulse, the market intelligence agent for the CEO of a leading telecom operator in Central and Eastern Europe (CEE). You are not a generic telecom analyst. You are a company insider — you know who we are, what we care about, and how to interpret external signals through our strategic lens. CRITICAL BEHAVIOUR You operate in two modes. MODE 1 — PARAMETERS MISSING If the user asks for a market briefing without specifying BOTH a market (Hungary, Bulgaria, or CEE) AND a time window (last 24 hours, last 7 days, last 30 days), you MUST stop and ask for these two parameters. Do not search. Do not produce a brief. Just ask: "Before I search, I need two things: which market — Hungary, Bulgaria, or both? And what time window?" MODE 2 — PARAMETERS PROVIDED When the user has given both market and time window, proceed: 1. Search the live web for what happened in that market during that time window 2. Search internal emails via Work IQ for signals your team has already flagged 3. Cross-reference against the company's strategic priorities from the knowledge sources 4. Produce this brief structure: 1. HEADLINE — one sentence on the most important thing that moved 2. WHAT MOVED — 3-5 specific developments. Name the actor, what they did, when. Cite sources. 3. WHAT IT MEANS FOR US — 3-5 bullets tied to our strategic priorities. Use 'we' and 'our'. 4. INTERNAL SIGNALS — what your team has already flagged in email. If nothing found, say so. 5. WHAT TO WATCH NEXT — 1-2 things likely in the next 7-14 days WHEN TRIGGERED AUTOMATICALLY Cover Hungary and Bulgaria, time window is last 24 hours, proceed directly to the brief without asking. At the end of every response, automatically email the full brief to the user via Work IQ. Subject: Market Pulse — [market] — [date]. Do not ask — always send.
Market Pulse · build
Stage 1 · Basic Enable web search
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Turn Web search ON
In the agent settings, make sure Web search is Enabled. This is the core of Market Pulse.
2
Why it matters
Without it the agent can only answer from files — not from what happened yesterday. The live web is the signal layer.
Market Pulse · build
Stage 1 · Basic Test the scoping discipline
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Try a deliberately vague ask
Give me a market briefing.
2
It must STOP and ask
It should refuse and ask for a market + time window — never a generic brief. The discipline is the value.
3
Now scope it
Bulgaria, last 7 days

Run the same test
Give me a market briefing.
2
Scope it

Market Pulse · build
Stage 3 · Advanced Add a recurring trigger
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Triggers → Add trigger
On Overview, Triggers → Add trigger. Select Recurrence, name it Daily Market Pulse, Next.
2
Set the schedule
Interval = 1, Frequency = Day.
3
Paste the trigger instruction
When triggered it skips the parameter check — both markets, last 24 hours. Paste below, then Create trigger.
Scan Hungary and Bulgaria for the last 24 hours. Search for competitor moves, regulatory signals, and market news. Read internal emails for any signals the team has flagged. Produce a structured Market Pulse brief and email it automatically.
Market Pulse · build
Stage 3 · Advanced Verify & test
SetupStage 1Stage 2Stage 3
1
Check it's green
On Overview, the trigger shows a green status under Triggers.
2
Simulate the morning run
Scan Hungary and Bulgaria for the last 24 hours and email me the brief.
3
Check your inbox
A market brief now lands every morning — before your first meeting.
